data.title: Honest Company rallies after showing turnaround momentum in Q2
data.description:
The Honest Company (NASDAQ:HNST) rallied in early trading on Friday after the retailer posted a beat-and-raise quarter.

The company reported that revenue was up 10% year-over-year to $93 million, driven by strong performance across the baby products and wipes portfolios. Gross margin increased to 38.3% of sales from 27.1% a year ago. The margin improvement was driven by improvements across the entire cost structure, including supply chain and product costs, as well as price increases and efficient trade spend.

Morgan Stanley noted the quarter featured solid progress and said it was encouraged by HNST's turnaround progress. Looking ahead, analyst Dara Mohsenian and his team and see a continued solid long-term growth runway, but kept HNST rated at Equal Weight with risk from macro and competitive pressure seen for the near term.

Telsey Advisory Group analyst Dana Telsey also noted that operating momentum appears to be building. "HNST’s recent top- and bottom-line results are indicative that the Transformation Initiative’s efforts are bearing fruit," she wrote.

Shares of Honest Company (HNST) were up 10.8% in the premarket trading session to $3.24 vs. the 52-week range of $1.06 to $4.89.

The Honest Company, founded in 2012 by Jessica Alba, Christopher Gavigan, Brian Lee, and Sean Kane, emerged from Alba's desire for safer consumer products after her own experiences with harsh chemicals during her first pregnancy. Initially offering baby and household products, the company quickly expanded its range, emphasizing eco-friendly and non-toxic ingredients. By 2015, it was valued at $1.7 billion, but faced challenges including lawsuits over product claims. Despite those hurdles, The Honest Company went public in 2021, raising $412.8 million in its IPO at $16 per share.

data.title: Acer reports Q2 results
data.description:
• Acer press release (OTC:ACEYY): Q2 revenues of NT$67.14 billion, up 15.2% year-on-year (YoY).
  • Net income was NT$1.40 billion with earnings-per-share NT$0.47.

data.title: Merck to buy experimental B-cell depletion therapy for $700M in cash upfront
data.description:
Merck (NYSE:MRK) has agreed to acquire an investigational B-cell depletion therapy, CN201, from Shanghai-based biopharma company Curon Biopharmaceutical for an upfront payment of $700M in cash.

CN201 is a clinical-stage bispecific antibody with potential applications in B-cell malignancies and autoimmune diseases. The drug is currently being evaluated in Phase 1 and Phase 1b/2 clinical trials for the treatment of relapsed or refractory non-Hodgkin’s lymphoma and relapsed or refractory B-cell acute lymphocytic leukemia, respectively.

Merck (MRK) will acquire full global rights to CN201 and incur a pre-tax charge of around $750M, reflecting the upfront payment, in the quarter that the transaction closes. The drugmaker plans to evaluate CN201 as a treatment for B-cell malignancies as well as investigate its potential to provide a novel, scalable option for the treatment of autoimmune diseases.

The transaction is expected to close in the third quarter of 2024 and be accounted for as an asset acquisition. Curon is also eligible to receive up to $600M in milestone payments associated with the development and regulatory approval of CN201.

data.title: P10 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.24 beats by $0.04, revenue of $71.1M beats by $5.22M
data.description:
• P10 press release (NYSE:PX): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.24 beats by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $71.1M (+13.8% Y/Y) beats by $5.22M.
  • Fee-Related Revenue: $68.3 million, a 12% increase year over year.
  • Fee-Paying Assets Under Management: $23.8 billion, an 8% increase year over year.
  • Adjusted EBITDA: $35.4 million, compared to $34.8 million in the prior year.

data.title: Earnings summary: New Fortress Energy sees FY24 adj. EBITDA of $1.4-1.5B, $1.3B for FY25
Granite Ridge Resources misses top-line and bottom-line estimates; updates FY24 outlook
data.description:
• Granite Ridge Resources press release (NYSE:GRNT): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.13 misses by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $90.65M (+3.5% Y/Y) misses by $1.94M.
  • Increased production by 7% to 23,106 barrels of oil equivalent per day (47% oil) from the second quarter of 2023.
  • Second quarter 2024 oil production volumes totaled 10,940 barrels per day, a 5% increase from the second quarter of 2023. Natural gas production for the second quarter of 2024 totaled 72,997 thousand cubic feet of natural gas per day, a 9% increase from the second quarter of 2023.
  • Generated $68.3 million of Adjusted EBITDAX (non-GAAP).
  • Placed 62 gross (9.10 net) wells online.

data.title: Construction Partners GAAP EPS of $0.59 beats by $0.05, revenue of $517.8M beats by $13.39M
data.description:
• Construction Partners press release (NASDAQ:ROAD): Q3 GAAP EPS of $0.59 beats by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $517.8M (+22.7% Y/Y) beats by $13.39M.

  • Record Backlog of $1.86 Billion

  • The Company is increasing guidance for fiscal 2024 with regard to revenue, net income, Adjusted EBITDA and Adjusted EBITDA Margin, as follows:

    • Revenue in the range of $1.835 billion to $1.860 billion
    • Net income in the range of $73.5 million to $76.5 million
    • Adjusted EBITDA in the range of $219 million to $228 million
    • Adjusted EBITDA Margin in the range of 11.9% to 12.3%

data.title: Berry Non-GAAP EPS of $0.18 misses by $0.02, revenue of $196.82M beats by $19.65M
data.description:
• Berry press release (NASDAQ:BRY): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.18 misses by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $196.82M (-14.2% Y/Y) beats by $19.65M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA of $74 million in the second quarter, a 7% increase over the first quarter of 2024, with Cash Flow from Operations totaling $71 million and Adjusted Free Cash Flow of $19 million.
  • Produced 25,300 Boe/d, flat to first quarter; above the midpoint of 2024 annual guidance of 25,200 boe/d.

data.title: Rocket Lab stock soars on record quarterly revenue, constellation plans
data.description:
Rocket Lab USA (NASDAQ:RKLB) shares jumped over 15% Friday morning after the aerospace company reported record quarterly revenues, led by strong demand for its launch services and space systems products.

“This year’s second quarter was Rocket Lab’s highest revenue quarter in company history at $106M,” CEO Peter Beck said. That implies a 15% quarter-on-quarter increase and a whopping 71% year-on-year increase.

The Launch Services segment delivered revenue of $29.4M, slightly above the company’s guidance, while the space system segment delivered $77M in the quarter, also in-line with its guidance.

The firm ended the quarter with $1.07B of total backlog, with launch backlog of $294M and space systems backlog of $772.6M. “Our current backlog continues to support our current year target average revenue per launch of $7.5M, with some quarterly variability tied to volume purchase discounts, launch location, and mission assurance requirements,” the management said in the earnings call.

Looking ahead, Rocket Lab (RKLB) expects Q3 revenue of $100M-$105M (consensus estimate: $110.54M) and adjusted gross margin of 30% to 32%. Non-GAAP gross margin was 30.7% in Q2, in-line with the company’s prior guidance range of 30% to 32%.

Beck also shared a glimpse of where Rocket Lab is headed in the future, particularly plans or a Rocket Lab-owned and operated satellite constellation which is part of the executive’s strategy to become an “end-to-end space company.”

“By owning launch and spacecraft, we're at a distinct advantage when it comes to establishing our own space capabilities or constellations. We can build and launch our own spacecraft at cost and we don't have to wait in-line for limited launch capacity,” Beck said.
